sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to make Cardano the title sponsor of Rare Evo 2026 main stage and global livestream and to support Rare Dev Gov Day 2026.

Deliverables: Cardano title sponsorship for Rare Evo 2026 main stage and livestream, Cardano title sponsorship for Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026, free General Admission access for the Cardano community, complimentary booth space for selected Cardano ecosystem projects, travel and lodging support for 20 Cardano ecosystem contributors or volunteers, five Cardano-focused main stage panel opportunities, Cardano workshops and governance sessions, livestream ads and global livestream participation, tier-1 media coverage using 2-3 selected vendors, return of 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury
Deadline/expiry: Rare Dev Gov Day on July 28, 2026 and Rare Evo 2026 on July 28-31, 2026 in Las Vegas

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Rare Network requests ₳2,750,000, described as $660,000 at a $0.24 ADA valuation, to fund two Cardano sponsorship packages for Rare Evo 2026 and Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The cost breakdown allocates ₳1,166,666.67 to Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026 sponsorship, ₳833,333.33 to Rare Evo 2026 title sponsorship, and ₳750,000 to Intersect, audit, fees, tax withholdings, and a Dev Gov Day 2027 venue deposit.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Rare Network says it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with the current retail value of a VIP ticket stated as $900.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Rare Evo 2026 will occur regardless of treasury funding, but the proposal says treasury funding would expand Cardano presence, accessibility, participation, and strategic industry outreach through existing event infrastructure.

Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal states Rare Evo 2025 had 2,500 attendees, 80 exhibitor booths, 85 speakers, 100+ ecosystem partners, 20K+ livestream views, 4M social impressions, 40+ Cardano projects represented, and 50+ enterprise participants.

Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): The proposal states Cardano Dev Gov Day 2025 was free and publicly accessible, welcomed approximately 500 attendees, and included a Charles Hoskinson keynote, governance discussions, technical education, workshops, and community activities.
missing: Independent evidence for: Rare Network says it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with the current retail value of a VIP ticket stated as $900.

Independent evidence for: Rare Evo 2026 will occur regardless of treasury funding, but the proposal says treasury funding would expand Cardano presence, accessibility, participation, and strategic industry outreach through existing event infrastructure.

Independent evidence for: Rare Network claims it has been active in the Cardano ecosystem since 2020 and has produced more than 60 blockchain industry side events and activations across conferences including ETHDenver, TOKEN2049, Consensus, Duke Blockchain Conference, Paris Blockchain Week, and Cardano Summit.

Independent evidence for: The sponsorship allocation may change if a different sponsor purchases the mainstage or livestream package before passage, in which case Cardano would receive a sponsorship of equal impact or dollar value such as Enterprise & Policy Social, Registration, Closeout Party, or College Row.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Rare Network requests ₳2,750,000, described as $660,000 at a $0.24 ADA valuation, to fund two Cardano sponsorship packages for Rare Evo 2026 and Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Rare Network says it will return 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury, with the current retail value of a VIP ticket stated as $900." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
